message:
perf: avoid allocation in normalize_line_endings_chunk when no CR bytes present

Return Cow::Borrowed(chunk) directly when no pending CR exists and no
\r byte is found via memchr, skipping the Vec allocation and byte-by-byte
copy entirely. This is the common case for LF-only streams (Linux, macOS,
most LLM provider output), reducing allocation pressure on every bash
output chunk.

When CR bytes are present, the existing normalization logic runs
unchanged and returns Cow::Owned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
